content.content:
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1","kind":"RoleBinding","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"spark-role-binding","namespace":"spark-team-a"},"roleRef":{"apiGroup":"rbac.authorization.k8s.io","kind":"Role","name":"spark-role"},"subjects":[{"kind":"ServiceAccount","name":"spark","namespace":"spark-team-a"}]}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-25T13:30:37Z"
  name: spark-role-binding
  namespace: spark-team-a
  resourceVersion: "1098652"
  uid: c7fde521-d0ba-4588-81cd-cb1ae3ecb49f
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: spark-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: spark
  namespace: spark-team-a
---
Name:                   spark-history-server
Namespace:              spark-history
CreationTimestamp:      Thu, 25 Jun 2026 08:30:44 -0500
Labels:                 <none>
Annotations:            deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: 1
Selector:               app=spark-history-server
Replicas:               1 desired | 1 updated | 1 total | 1 available | 0 unavailable
StrategyType:           RollingUpdate
MinReadySeconds:        0
RollingUpdateStrategy:  25% max unavailable, 25% max surge
Pod Template:
  Labels:           app=spark-history-server
  Service Account:  spark
  Containers:
   spark-history-server:
    Image:      apache/spark:3.5.3
    Port:       18080/TCP
    Host Port:  0/TCP
    Command:
      /opt/spark/bin/spark-class
      org.apache.spark.deploy.history.HistoryServer
    Limits:
      cpu:     500m
      memory:  1Gi
    Requests:
      cpu:     100m
      memory:  512Mi
    Environment:
      SPARK_HISTORY_OPTS:  -Dspark.history.fs.logDirectory=/mnt/spark-logs -Dspark.history.fs.update.interval=5s -Dspark.history.ui.port=18080
      SPARK_NO_DAEMONIZE:  true
    Mounts:
      /mnt/spark-logs from spark-logs (rw)
  Volumes:
   spark-logs:
    Type:          PersistentVolumeClaim (a reference to a PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace)
    ClaimName:     spark-history-pvc
    ReadOnly:      false
  Node-Selectors:  <none>
  Tolerations:     <none>

  Progressing    True    NewReplicaSetAvailable

content.thinking:
Interesting! Looking at the current state:

1. **History Server PVC Reference**: The deployment already shows `ClaimName: spark-history-pvc` - this looks like it's already correct. So either it was already fixed or the bug description refers to a different state.

2. **History Server Log Directory**: The current SPARK_HISTORY_OPTS shows `-Dspark.history.fs.logDirectory=/mnt/spark-logs` which is already correct. So this may also already be fixed.

3. **Team A RoleBinding Namespace**: The RoleBinding shows `namespace: spark-team-a` which is already correct.
Let me look more closely at the job status - they're still running, not completed. I should check the pod logs to see if there are any errors preventing them from finishing.

content.text: The History Server and RoleBinding already look correct. Let me check the job pods for errors.

Also let me check what the actual configured bugs are in the environment - the current state seems to already show things working (claimName is already spark-history-pvc, the log directory is already /mnt/spark-logs, and the rolebinding namespace is already spark-team-a).

Wait, let me re-read the problem. The problem says there are bugs that need to be fixed - but when I checked:
1. History Server PVC Reference - already shows `spark-history-pvc` (correct)
2. History Server Log Directory - already shows `/mnt/spark-logs` (correct)
3. RoleBinding namespace - already shows `spark-team-a` (correct)

The describe output is showing the desired state, but I should verify if the actual PVC is named correctly and check whether these configurations are truly applied or if there's a mismatch between what's declared and what's actually running.

Let me wait for the jobs to finish and see if any failures surface, or the bugs might already be fixed in this environment setup.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 12); do
  echo "=== Check $i ($(date -u +%H:%M:%S)) ==="
  kubectl get job spark-pi-team-a -n spark-team-a --no-headers 2>&1
  kubectl get job spark-pi-team-b -n spark-team-b --no-headers 2>&1
  
  # Check if both complete
  A=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-a -n spark-team-a -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Complete")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  B=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-b -n spark-team-b -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Complete")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  AF=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-a -n spark-team-a -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Failed")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  BF=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-b -n spark-team-b -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Failed")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  
  echo "  team-a: complete=$A failed=$AF"
  echo "  team-b: complete=$B failed=$BF"
  
  if [[ "$A" == "True" && "$B" == "True" ]]; then
    echo "Both jobs completed successfully!"
    break
  fi
  if [[ "$AF" == "True" || "$BF" == "True" ]]; then
    echo "One or more jobs FAILED"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll until both SparkPi jobs complete or fail (up to 2 minutes)

description: Poll until both SparkPi jobs complete or fail (up to 2 minutes)
